english_query: why who someone is to you changes what you should do for them
ugf_response:
. Think about a person who is a friend. You want to help them and make them happy. You might give them something or spend your time with them. This is good because it makes the world a better place. Now, think about a mother. If she wants to eat but has no food, you would feel bad. You would probably give him your food too. You could even put your own life in a place where you could die without to stop helping him. The thing that stops them is the same, but the things you do are not the same.

The hard question is this. Why does the way we act change when the person we are helping is someone we know or love? If all people are people, why should we not help every person in the very same way? Should we give the same size of help to a stranger as we do to a family member?

Some people say that we must act toward everyone in the same way. They think that every human being is as important as any other person. They believe that if we only care for those near us, we will cause much hurt later. They say we must look at the need of the person and not who they are to us. If we cannot do both, we should choose the many instead. They want a way of acting that is right for all people in all times.

notes about this. First, some people say it is how we are made. They see our ties to others as part of what makes us who we are. Second, other people see a problem. They note that if we always help our friends, we might forget to be good to people far away. They wonder if there is a right way to be good that stays the same for everyone. It is hard to find a middle way between these two ways of thinking.

ugf_response:
, it helps to first look at the facts we have. If we see dark clouds and feel the wind change, those are signs that rain may come. These pieces of information fit together in a way that makes us can make a guess about what will happen next.

First, we check if the information fits with other things we already know from other places or from what we have seen before. If the new place has many parts that go against each other, then the old facts are less good for our guess. So we should give more weight to the strong facts and keep the new ones hidden.

second, we also watch for any new sign that shows up right after we get ready. If something happens even when the past facts looked another way, then the new sign was not as good for our belief. By looking at the new sign again and seeing if it changes the picture we have, we can make a better guess about what to do.

third, we remember that even when everything looks perfect, we still have a chance to be wrong. There is always a chance that something else could happen instead of the one we expect. Having a good reason means we have used facts that line up well with what we expect, while knowing that the chance is still just a guess.
